ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin / Agenda safe-mode reboot via bcdedit prior to encryption
ucs.description: Hunts the Qilin (Agenda) ransomware signature behaviour of reconfiguring the Windows boot loader to safeboot (minimal/network) and then forcibly rebooting so the encryptor runs in an environment where most EDR / AV agents are not loaded. Known affiliate operator playbook for Qilin (a.k.a. Agenda, formerly tracked under that name by Trend Micro / Microsoft) — first publicly documented by Sophos/Group-IB in 2023 and re-observed in the Qilin.B variant in 2024.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name=bcdedit.exe (Processes.process="*safeboot*minimal*" OR Processes.process="*safeboot*network*" OR (Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*" AND Processes.process="*no*")) NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=30m | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=left dest [| tstats `summariesonly` count as rebootCount values(Processes.process) as rebootCmd min(_time) as rebootTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN (shutdown.exe,wininit.exe) (Processes.process="* /r *" OR Processes.process="*-r *" OR Processes.process="*/f*") by Processes.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`] | where rebootTime>=firstTime AND rebootTime<=lastTime+1800 | table firstTime,dest,user,cmdline,parent,rebootCmd,rebootTime
ucs.kql:
let SafebootSet = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has "safeboot"
         and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("minimal","network")
      or (ProcessCommandLine has "recoveryenabled" and ProcessCommandLine has "no")
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | project SafebootTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              SafebootCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              SafebootParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              SafebootParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let ForcedReboot = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where FileName in~ ("shutdown.exe","wininit.exe","shutdown.com")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (" /r"," -r")
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (" /f"," -f"," /t ")
    | project RebootTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RebootCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
SafebootSet
| join kind=inner ForcedReboot on DeviceId
| where RebootTime between (SafebootTime .. SafebootTime + 30m)
| project SafebootTime, RebootTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', RebootTime, SafebootTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          SafebootCmd, SafebootParent, SafebootParentCmd, RebootCmd
| order by SafebootTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let SafebootSet = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName endswith "bcdedit.exe"
    | where (CommandLine has "safeboot" and CommandLine has_any ("minimal","network"))
         or (CommandLine has "recoveryenabled" and CommandLine has "no")
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | project SafebootTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              SafebootCmd = CommandLine, SafebootParent = ParentProcessName;
let ForcedReboot = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where NewProcessName has_any ("shutdown.exe","wininit.exe")
    | where CommandLine has_any (" /r"," -r")
         and CommandLine has_any (" /f"," -f"," /t ")
    | project RebootTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, RebootCmd = CommandLine;
SafebootSet
| join kind=inner ForcedReboot on Computer
| where RebootTime between (SafebootTime .. SafebootTime + 30m)
| project SafebootTime, RebootTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', RebootTime, SafebootTime),
          Computer, Account, SafebootCmd, SafebootParent, RebootCmd
| order by SafebootTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Qilin/Agenda Ransomware Safe Mode Boot Reconfiguration
id: 8c2f0c4b-2f4e-4f1f-9d8a-7d6f1f2a3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects bcdedit.exe being used to enable safeboot (minimal/network) or disable recovery, a hallmark of Qilin (a.k.a. Agenda) ransomware which reboots into safe mode to evade EDR before encrypting.
references:
  - https://news.sophos.com/en-us/2024/07/11/qilin-ransomware-caught-stealing-credentials-stored-in-google-chrome/
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/24/j/the-evolution-of-qilin-ransomware.html
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026-05-03
tags:
  - attack.impact
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.t1562.009
  - attack.t1486
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    - Image|endswith: '\bcdedit.exe'
    - OriginalFileName: 'bcdedit.exe'
  selection_safeboot:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'safeboot'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'minimal'
      - 'network'
  selection_recovery:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'recoveryenabled'
      - 'no'
  filter_user:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and (selection_safeboot or selection_recovery) and not filter_user
falsepositives:
  - Helpdesk troubleshooting; legitimate use is rare and almost always preceded by a ticket
level: critical
ucs.rationale: Safe-mode-reboot-then-encrypt is one of the few publicly attributed signature behaviours of Qilin/Agenda. Other ransomware families (Snatch, BlackByte) have used the technique sporadically, but Qilin's affiliates use it as a documented step in their playbook (Sophos, Group-IB, Microsoft writeups). The combination of bcdedit safeboot + a forced /r /f /t 0 reboot inside a 30-minute window is excepti

ucs.title: [LLM] Qilin (Agenda) configuration-driven mass service kill + shadow-copy wipe
ucs.description: Catches the Qilin/Agenda 'preparation' phase: the Rust/Go binary takes a JSON config that lists backup, AV, EDR and DB services to terminate (Veeam, MSSQL, Exchange, Defender, Sophos, SentinelOne, etc.), then deletes Volume Shadow Copies via vssadmin/wmic and disables boot recovery — all within a tight time-window on the same host. Catching the chain (mass kill -> shadow wipe) is far stronger than either step alone.
ucs.splunk: | tstats `summariesonly` count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN (net.exe,net1.exe,sc.exe,taskkill.exe,powershell.exe,pwsh.exe)) (Processes.process="*veeam*" OR Processes.process="*backup*" OR Processes.process="*sql*" OR Processes.process="*exchange*" OR Processes.process="*defender*" OR Processes.process="*sophos*" OR Processes.process="*cylance*" OR Processes.process="*carbonblack*" OR Processes.process="*sentinel*" OR Processes.process="*crowdstrike*" OR Processes.process="*kavfs*" OR Processes.process="*mssecsvc*" OR Processes.process="*acronis*" OR Processes.process="*trendmicro*") NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM") by Processes.dest Processes.user _time span=10m | rename Processes.* as * | where count>=5 | join type=inner dest [| tstats `summariesonly` min(_time) as vssTime values(Processes.process) as vssCmd from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name=vssadmin.exe Processes.process="*delete*shadows*") OR (Processes.process_name=wmic.exe Processes.process="*shadowcopy*delete*") OR (Processes.process_name=bcdedit.exe Processes.process="*recoveryenabled*no*") by Processes.dest | rename Processes.* as *] | where vssTime>=_time AND vssTime<=_time+1800 | table _time,dest,user,count,vssTime,vssCmd
ucs.kql:
let _targeted = dynamic(["veeam","backup","sql","exchange","defender","sense","sophos","cylance","carbonblack","sentinel","crowdstrike","kavfs","mssecsvc","acronis","trendmicro","vss","sqlbrowser","sqlwriter","msexchange","mepocs"]);
let KillBurst = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (FileName in~ ("net.exe","net1.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "stop")
         or (FileName =~ "sc.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("stop","delete","config"))
         or (FileName =~ "taskkill.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "/f")
         or (FileName in~ ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has_any ("Stop-Service","Stop-Process"))
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_targeted)
    | summarize KillCount = count(),
                KilledTargets = make_set(ProcessCommandLine, 30),
                FirstKill = min(Timestamp),
                LastKill  = max(Timestamp)
        by DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, bin(Timestamp, 10m)
    | where KillCount >= 5;
let VssWipe = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(7d)
    | where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
         or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
         or (FileName =~ "bcdedit.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "recoveryenabled" and ProcessCommandLine has "no")
         or (FileName =~ "wbadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("delete","catalog"))
    | project VssTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, VssCmd = ProcessCommandLine, VssBin = FileName;
KillBurst
| join kind=inner VssWipe on DeviceId
| where VssTime between (FirstKill - 30m .. LastKill + 30m)
| project DeviceName, AccountName, FirstKill, LastKill,
          KillCount, KilledTargets, VssTime, VssBin, VssCmd
| order by FirstKill desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let _targeted = dynamic(["veeam","backup","sql","exchange","defender","sense","sophos","cylance","carbonblack","sentinel","crowdstrike","kavfs","mssecsvc","acronis","trendmicro","vss","sqlbrowser","sqlwriter","msexchange","mepocs"]);
let KillBurst = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where (NewProcessName has_any ("\\net.exe","\\net1.exe") and CommandLine has "stop")
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\sc.exe" and CommandLine has_any ("stop","delete","config"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\taskkill.exe" and CommandLine has "/f")
         or (NewProcessName has_any ("\\powershell.exe","\\pwsh.exe") and CommandLine has_any ("Stop-Service","Stop-Process"))
    | where CommandLine has_any (_targeted)
    | summarize KillCount = count(),
                KilledTargets = make_set(CommandLine, 30),
                FirstKill = min(TimeGenerated),
                LastKill  = max(TimeGenerated)
        by Computer, Account, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
    | where KillCount >= 5;
let VssWipe = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where (NewProcessName endswith "\\vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("delete","shadows"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\wmic.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("shadowcopy","delete"))
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\bcdedit.exe" and CommandLine has "recoveryenabled" and CommandLine has "no")
         or (NewProcessName endswith "\\wbadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("delete","catalog"))
    | project VssTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, VssCmd = CommandLine, VssBin = NewProcessName;
KillBurst
| join kind=inner VssWipe on Computer
| where VssTime between (FirstKill - 30m .. LastKill + 30m)
| project Computer, Account, FirstKill, LastKill,
          KillCount, KilledTargets, VssTime, VssBin, VssCmd
| order by FirstKill desc
ucs.rationale: Qilin/Agenda ships with a JSON-driven config (publicly reverse-engineered by Trend Micro and Group-IB) that hard-codes a service-kill list overwhelmingly weighted toward Veeam, MSSQL, Exchange, and EDR/AV agents — followed almost immediately by VSS/recovery wipe. Generic 'service stopped' or 'vssadmin delete shadows' detections fire constantly; requiring 5+ targeted kills inside 10 minutes plus a
